DISCOVERY 302, A R G U E D; DEFENDANTS CHRISTINA LY, RAYMOND YOUNG, TINA YEUNG'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER DISCOVERY RESPONSES AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS FROM PLAINTIFF'S AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $3,670.00 ARE AWARDED IN FAVOR OF DEFENDANTS AND AGAINST PLAINTIFFS AND THEIR COUNSEL. SAID SUM SHALL BE DUE AND PAYABLE TO THE LAW OFFICES OF MURPHY, PEARSON, BRADLEY & FEENEY WITHIN 20 DAYS OF THE NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. ON ITS OWN MOTION, THE COURT ORDERS THE DOCUMENTS NUMBERED FEL0001 THROUGH FEL0009 INCLUDED IN EXHIBIT T TO THE DECLARATION OF KIMBERLY A. SHIELDS IN SUPPORT OF THE MOTION TO BE SEALED, AS THEY APPEAR TO BE PRIVATE MEDICAL RECORDS. ORDER SIGNED. SEE SIGNED ORDER FOR FULL TEXT. JIM WEIXEL, JUDGE PRO-TEM, JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, CLERK, NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Leave To Amend The Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-26-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFs ALEX FELSINGER and ANDREW ZOLLMAN's Motion For Leave To Amend The Complaint is granted, for good cause shown. The trial is continued to October 28, 2013 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 206. The discovery cut-off will be based on the new trial date. Counsel are to meet and confer in advance of the hearing (if any) to agree on the trial continuance date if October 28, 2013 is not mutually acceptable. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

Off Calendar Mar-11-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs CHRISTINE ADAMS & HEART OF THE CITY FARMERS MARKET's Motion To Bifurcate By Defendants is Denied, without prejudice, to the matter being raised before the trial judge. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: A. James Robertson; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Production Of Documents Dfrom Pltf'S And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-31-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Derek St. Pierre, 415.441.5711, appearing for the Plaintiff;
Kimberly A. Shields, Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney, 415.788.1900, appearing for the Defendants;
Rowena Seto, Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith, 415.362.2580, appear by Court Call for Defendants Christine Adams & heart of the City Farmers Market.

Ruling: A R G U E D; DEFENDANTS CHRISTINA LY, RAYMOND YOUNG, TINA YEUNG'S Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Production Of Documents from Plaintiff's And Request For Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED. Defendant's request for monetary sanctions is GRANTED. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $3,670.00 are awarded in favor of Defendants and against Plaintiffs and their counsel. Said sum shall be due and payable to the law offices of Murphy, Pearson, Bradley & Feeney within 20 days of the Notice of Entry of this Order. On its own motion, the Court orders the documents numbered FEL0001 through FEL0009 included in Exhibit T to the Declaration of Kimberly A. Shields in support of the motion to be sealed, as they appear to be private medical records. Order signed. See signed order for full text. Jim Weixel, Judge Pro-Tem, Jacqueline Alameda, Clerk, Not Reported. =302/JPT
